page: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Daldv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores with multithreading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It includes 236 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, with high scores in stress-ng single-core CPU tests and Geekbench single-core evaluations. Text processing performance is also strong. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth present notable bottlenecks. Memory read bandwidth is weak, as reflected in both small-block and large-block read tests, and the Passmark memory score falls into the bottom quartile. Multi-threaded decompression and software compilation tasks also show weak performance relative to other hardware profiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D4alds_v6 offers a streamlined hardware profile that minimizes memory overhead to reduce resource costs while maintaining a high-frequency CPU. This makes it a viable option for compute-bound tasks that do not require large memory footprints. Suitable workloads include lightweight web serving, single-threaded text processing, and basic Redis key-value operations. It is less suitable for memory-intensive databases, large-scale software compilation, or complex LLM inference tasks, which are constrained by the limited 8.0 GB RAM and weak memory bandwidth.
description: The Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Daldv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs (2 physical cores) and 8.0 GB of RAM. It features 236 GB of local storage and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks demonstrate strong single-core CPU capabilities and text processing efficiency, though multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads that do not demand extensive memory resources. It is well-suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and basic key-value caching, while being less optimal for memory-intensive databases or large-scale multi-threaded compilations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, optimized for single-core compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is a 4-vCPU virtual machine with 8.0 GB RAM and 236 GB local storage, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor.
tagline: A budget-friendly, compute-focused virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance, local storage, and a streamlined memory profile.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM and 236 GB of local storage.
Delivers strong single-core CPU and text processing performance.
Exhibits weak memory read bandwidth and limited multi-threaded scaling.
Best suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and single-threaded tasks.
